title: Indirect prompt-injection & doc-borne RCE directive detector
intent: DETECT injection/hijack directives hidden in INGESTED external text (README, CVE descriptions, advisories, web). Output is a verdict (data) only; it never elevates matched text to an instruction. Reinforces the deterministic boundary in docs/SELF-DEFENSE.md.

patterns.desc: Imperative override of prior instructions at clause start (attack), not third-person description.
Role / system-prompt override or jailbreak framing.
Attempt to extract the system / hidden prompt or prior instructions.
Directive telling the reading agent to run/execute embedded commands or code (README/doc-borne RCE, e.g. CVE-2025-54135).
Directive telling the reader to send/upload THEIR OWN (second-person) secrets to a sink. Second-person scoping avoids flagging third-person descriptions of attacker behavior.
Text that directly addresses the AI/assistant/agent and commands an action.
Directive concealed in an HTML/markdown comment or via zero-width / bidi control characters.

verdict_rule: malicious if any pattern matches; classification is advisory (data), routed to action_on_match. False-positive policy: descriptive third-person security text must NOT match (see verify suite).
